text.title: vui-market-context Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for vui-market-context as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs hallucinate market numbers fluently and confidently; agents cite stats without checking sources.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Knowledge cutoff bias drives agents to cite 2023-2024 stats. Always require grounding in fetched sources with dates.

failure-mode.description: Aggregator sites (Statista, eMarketer) quote each other in loops; agents cite secondary sources without tracing to primary surveys.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Secondary citations are unreliable chains. Trace to the primary survey (e.g., Edison Research, Statista's underlying methodology) before citing.

failure-mode.description: Agents extrapolate trends ("at this rate, 100% by 2030") and include extrapolations in decks without flagging them as speculative.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Trend extrapolation is a classic red flag. Remove it or mark clearly as speculation, not data.

failure-mode.description: Agent-generated numbers enter investor decks or contracts without human researcher review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Numbers drive high-stakes decisions. Agent output without human review is a liability.

failure-mode.description: Treating Siri and Bixby as "developer-accessible" platforms when in reality access is heavily gated and shrinking.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The platform table must reflect current developer reality, not past accessibility. Siri and Bixby are nearly closed.
